notes: THE DEVIL-unique action cards joined into the shared tableau when the Devil arcana is in play. Five verbs that the room offers: drink, rack, play a song, pay your tab, and the one the room hopes you don't notice — walking out. SETTLE UP is the win-side verb: the only card that lowers CHAIN.

cards.title: ANOTHER ROUND
cards.flavor: Tap the bar. Lou pours. The shoulders drop a half-inch. The chain doesn't move; it tightens.

effects.text: Another round. Lou doesn't say anything.

cards.title: RACK 'EM
cards.flavor: Set the table, grab a cue. The pool table only counts as not-drinking until the 8-ball doesn't sink.

then.text: Someone wandered over. You hand them the cue without breaking eye contact with the felt.

else.text: You rack alone. The 8-ball settles in the triangle like a held breath.

cards.title: FEED THE JUKE
cards.flavor: Slip a quarter in. Pick the song. The juke will keep playing the kind of song you picked all night, whether you keep feeding it or not.

effects.text: The song catches. Your hands warm around the glass.

cards.title: SETTLE UP
cards.flavor: Slide the bills across the brass clip. Lou doesn't count them in front of you. He never does. That's part of the trick.

then.text: Tab settled. Lou nods. The back alley is suddenly closer than the front door.

else.text: Bills across the brass clip. Lou taps the wood once. There's more.

cards.title: WALK OUT
cards.flavor: Six steps. The geometry of leaving. You will not be invited back; the bar's hospitality is the chain, not the welcome.

effects.text: Six steps. The neon Schlitz hum drops out of your ears between the fourth step and the fifth.
